key_insight: A deep learning pipeline can quantify DLBCL tumor microenvironment features and reveal subtype-specific differences between GCB and ABC tumors, including stronger immune-cell involvement and ABC–M2 macrophage interactions.
clinical_relevance: These tumor microenvironment signatures could help refine DLBCL risk stratification beyond the Hans classifier and support more detailed subtype characterization in precision oncology.
method: Unbiased deep learning analysis of multiplexed tumor images from a DLBCL patient cohort
Quantification of cell composition, morphology, and spatial organization in the tumor microenvironment
Comparison of microenvironment features between GCB and ABC/non-GCB tumors
evidence_signal: The abstract reports cohort-based image analysis showing altered cell population proportions and preferential ABC interaction with M2 macrophages, but it does not provide performance metrics or validation results.
watch_item: Findings are based on image-derived associations from a patient cohort, so broader validation and standardization would be needed before clinical implementation.
paper_title: Characterization of DLBCL cell of origin-phenotypes based on tumor microenvironment features
